brief.headline: Synchronized global monetary tightening — Fed erased all 2026 cut projections, BOJ hiked to 1.0% (a 30-year high), and May PCE reaccelerated to 4.10% YoY, while the US–Iran peace deal began slowly deflating the energy shock that triggered the inflation surge.

regime.label: Mixed — Convergent global tightening
regime.evidence: DXY at 101.34 (1-year high); gold −26% from peak; Fed/ECB/BOJ all hawkish simultaneously

events.title: FOMC Hawkish Dot-Plot Reversal
events.reaction: DXY +1% to 101.34; 2Y at 4.10%; gold →~$4,000; Oct 2026 hike priced
events.detail: FOMC held at 3.50–3.75% unanimously but eliminated all 2026 cut projections — 9/18 members now project at least one hike. Chair Warsh dropped the easing-bias language entirely, marking the sharpest forward-guidance hawkish pivot since 2022.

events.title: May PCE Reacceleration (4.10% YoY)
events.reaction: Confirmed Oct hike pricing; DXY held 101; gold under pressure at ~$4,000
events.detail: May PCE rose to 4.10% YoY (from 3.80%) with Core at 3.40%; May CPI at 4.17% with 3-month annualized pace at 8.2%, validating the Fed's hawkish pivot and closing the door on any 2026 dovish reversal.

events.title: BOJ Hike to 1.0% — 30-Year High
events.reaction: USD/JPY pressured; JGB yields rose; carry unwind risk elevated
events.detail: BOJ hiked 25bps to 1.00% (highest since 1995) on a 7-1 vote with explicit guidance for further hikes toward ~2% neutral; combined with Fed and ECB tightening, global carry trade spread compression accelerates.

events.title: US–Iran Peace / Hormuz Reopening
events.reaction: WTI −4.8% to $80.75; Brent −4.7% to $83.17; gold fell; shipping rallied
events.detail: US–Iran MOU signed June 18 formally ended the conflict and reopened the Strait of Hormuz; oil fell >4% on signing day but full supply normalization will take 3–6 months, keeping WTI ~$17–20 above pre-conflict levels.

events.title: US–China Tariff Pause + DOD Entity List
events.reaction: Mixed; Alibaba/Baidu ADRs volatile; MOFCOM retaliation warning; 60-day binary risk
events.detail: 60-day tariff pause at 30% offset by DOD adding 188 Chinese entities (Alibaba, Baidu, BYD) to its military company list; China's MOFCOM warned of 'resolute retaliation,' creating a hard mid-August binary event.

brief.headline: Geopolitical resolution meets a synchronized hawkish pivot — oil −33% from its wartime peak as the ECB, BOJ, and Fed all turned hawkish in a single week.

regime.label: Cautious risk-on
regime.evidence: S&P 11 of 12 weeks up, but DXY at a 1-year high

events.title: US-Iran peace deal / Hormuz reopening
events.reaction: WTI ~$77 (−33% from peak); S&P +0.9%; gold −6.8% MTD
events.detail: Pakistan announced a US-Iran MOU to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz; tankers began exiting and Brent fell from a ~$120 wartime peak toward ~$80. A canceled Switzerland signing left some fragility.

events.title: ECB +25bps — first hike in 3 years
events.reaction: EZ sovereign yields +25bps; Euro Stoxx pressured
events.detail: The ECB raised all three key rates 25bps, reversing eight consecutive cuts — its sharpest reversal since 2011 — as the energy shock pushed euro-area CPI to 3.2%.

events.title: FOMC hawkish dot-plot flip (Warsh era)
events.reaction: DXY ~100.8 (1-yr high); equities recovered
events.detail: The FOMC held at 3.50–3.75% but the median 2026 dot rose to 3.8%, flipping from an implied cut to an implied hike; traders repriced an October hike.

events.title: BOJ hikes to 1.0% — 30-year high
events.reaction: JPY strengthened; JGB yields at multi-decade highs
events.detail: The BOJ raised to 1.00% in a 7-1 vote, the highest since 1995, with core CPI above 2% for 44 straight months; the yen carry unwind accelerates.

events.title: SpaceX $75B IPO — largest in history
events.reaction: SPCX +19% day-1; Nasdaq +2.4% week
events.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) priced at $135, raised $75B, and jumped +19% day-1; combined with an Intel-Apple chip deal it drove the S&P's 11th up week in 12.

brief.headline: The dominant regime was sharply risk-off early in the week, followed by a violent risk-on reversal by June 11 — one of the most volatile weekly swings of 2026. US-Iran confrontation and subsequent peace-deal signals were the defining event: Nasdaq shed 4.18% (~$1T) on June 5 before recovering +2.54% on June 11, while May CPI hit 4.2% YoY.

regime.label: Mixed — Volatile risk-off then risk-on (Stagflationary)
regime.evidence: VIX surged June 5, partially retreated June 11; credit spreads widened then tightened; gold held $4,200 even on risk-on day

events.title: US-Iran Conflict / Peace Deal Signals
events.reaction: Nasdaq −4.18% → +2.54%; Gold ↑ toward $4,200/oz
events.detail: Escalating US-Iran tensions triggered a ~$1T global equity wipeout on June 5 (largest single-day loss since April 2025) and pushed May CPI to 4.2% YoY. On June 11, Trump signaled an imminent peace deal, catalyzing a sharp reversal across risk assets.

events.title: ECB Surprise +25 bps Rate Hike
events.reaction: EUR strengthened; peripheral spreads widened modestly
events.detail: The ECB raised its deposit rate 25bps to 2.25% — its first hike since 2023 — catching markets expecting a hold or cut, citing Iran-driven energy inflation. It revised 2026 inflation up to 3.0% and trimmed GDP growth to 0.8%.

events.title: Nasdaq Semiconductor Crash (~$1T)
events.detail: The Nasdaq fell 4.18% on June 5 (largest session decline since April 2025), erasing ~$1T intraday on three stacked catalysts: strong payrolls, a third-party AI capability report questioning semiconductor demand, and a large Meta secondary offering.

events.title: US-China 60-Day Tariff Pause
events.reaction: Risk-on rally; structural damage remains
events.detail: The Trump administration announced a 60-day pause on tariffs above 30% on Chinese goods, pulling back from the 145% peak, though a 30% baseline remains. US imports from China are at depths unseen since 2009 and the IMF cut global growth to 2.8%.

events.title: Fed Hold + Warsh Hawkish Signal
events.reaction: 99.5% hold priced; USD firm; yields elevated
events.detail: Markets priced a 99.5% probability of no change at the June 16-17 FOMC (hold at 3.50–3.75%), but new Chair Warsh's commentary signaled a departure from easing bias and openness to H2 2026 hikes, with CPI at 4.2% reinforcing the stance.

catalysts: Q2 Earnings Season (NVIDIA, Apple, banks) · July
ECB July Meeting · follow-up to surprise hike

brief.headline: The week was defined by the Iran war's structural grip on energy markets — now in its 100th day with ceasefire talks stalled — alongside a sharp late-week reversal driven by Broadcom's weak AI chip guidance and a blowout May jobs report. The dominant regime is stagflationary, with WTI ~$91 sustaining CPI at 3.8% and 172K jobs pushing year-end hike odds to 70%.

regime.label: Mixed — Stagflationary (risk-on then risk-off)
regime.evidence: S&P 500 all-time high 7,600 on June 2 and Q1 EPS growth 28.6%, but Nasdaq −4.7% for the week and gold falling on real-rate rise

events.title: Iran War / Strait of Hormuz Oil Supply Disruption
events.reaction: WTI +50% pre-war; US CPI 3.8%; stagflation risk
events.detail: The IEA calls the Iran war the largest supply disruption in oil-market history, with the Strait of Hormuz (~20–25% of global oil) effectively closed at its 100-day mark. CPI is at 3.8% YoY via a 17.9% energy surge, and ceasefire talks have stalled with no meaningful progress.

events.title: May 2026 NFP Blowout — 172K vs 80K Consensus
events.reaction: 10Y yield +6bps → 4.544%; USD surge; gold −2%
events.detail: May payrolls printed 172,000 jobs — a 115% beat vs the 80,000 consensus — with unemployment holding at 4.3%. The hot print sharply reduced rate-cut odds and pushed CME year-end hike probability to 70%.

events.title: Broadcom Q2 AI Chip Guidance Miss / Semis Rout
events.reaction: AVGO −12% AH; Nasdaq −4.18%; S&P −2.64%
events.detail: Broadcom posted record Q2 revenue of $22.19B but its Q3 AI chip guidance of $16B missed the $17.2B consensus by 7%, and a held (not raised) $56B full-year AI target read as a ceiling. AVGO fell 12% after-hours, triggering sector-wide contagion.

events.title: ECB June 11 Rate Hike — 99% Market Probability
events.reaction: EUR under pressure; 25bps hike to 2.25% fully priced
events.detail: Markets priced a 99% probability of a 25bps ECB hike to 2.25% at the June 11 meeting as Eurozone inflation rose to 3.0% on Iran-driven energy pass-through. At least one further hike is priced for H2 2026.

events.title: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as Fed Chair (Hawkish Shift)
events.reaction: 70% year-end hike odds; hawkish regime shift signal
events.detail: Kevin Warsh, a known hawkish monetarist, was confirmed as Fed Chair ahead of the June 16-17 FOMC, replacing Powell. Combined with 3.8% CPI and the NFP blowout, year-end hike probability jumped to 70%.

brief.headline: The dominant regime is stagflation-adjacent risk-on: equities powered through a 9th consecutive weekly gain (S&P 500 at 7,580; Dow's first close above 51,000), yet the macro backdrop is hostile — PCE at a 3-year high, a hawkish Fed transition, and a war-disrupted Strait of Hormuz. NVIDIA's record $81.6B quarter provided the dominant risk-on counterforce.

regime.label: Mixed — Stagflation-adjacent risk-on
regime.evidence: VIX subdued; 9 consecutive weekly S&P gains; Nasdaq +8% MTD; Dow first-ever above 51,000 — but saving rate 2.6%, PCE at 3-year high, record FOMC dissents

events.title: Iran War — Oil Shock & Ceasefire Volatility
events.reaction: WTI −19% May; Brent $91.89; Gold +43% YTD
events.detail: US-Iran retaliatory strikes on May 28 failed to sustain any oil rally; with a 60-day ceasefire MOU 'mostly agreed,' Brent collapsed −20% from its ~$112 peak. The Strait of Hormuz remains well below pre-war shipping volumes despite the nominal ceasefire.

events.title: FOMC Minutes Hawkish Shift + Warsh Era Begins
events.reaction: Cuts priced out; 10Y at 4.45%; hike risk rising
events.detail: Kevin Warsh took office May 15 as the 17th Fed Chair, and the April FOMC minutes (released May 20) revealed a record four dissents and left the door open to hikes. Market pricing for 2026 rate cuts was fully erased, with ~20% odds of a June hike.

events.title: PCE April +3.8% YoY — 3-Year High
events.reaction: Monthly +0.4% missed 0.5% est; muted equity reaction
events.detail: Headline PCE hit +3.8% YoY (highest since May 2023) and Core PCE +3.3%, though the monthly +0.4% missed the +0.5% consensus. The personal saving rate collapsed to 2.6%, the lowest since June 2022, signaling consumers funding spending via drawdowns.

events.title: NVIDIA Q1 FY27 Record $81.6B Revenue
events.reaction: NVDA +18%; Nasdaq +8% MTD
events.detail: NVIDIA beat on every metric (revenue $81.6B, Data Center $75.2B +92% YoY, Networking +199% YoY) and guided Q2 to $91B, validating the AI super-cycle. NVDA rose +18% post-earnings, anchoring the broad risk-on tape.

events.title: US-China Trade Truce & Tariff Extension
events.reaction: Reduced tail risk; Yuan stable; agri firmed
events.detail: A May 12 partial truce and the May 14 Trump-Xi summit produced commitments on farm/aircraft purchases and a tariff-exclusion extension to Nov 10, pulling back from the 104% baseline. Rare earth and semiconductor controls remain unresolved.

catalysts: NVIDIA Q2 FY27 Earnings ($91B bar) · August

brief.headline: The dominant regime is stagflationary risk-off: the 87-day-old Iran war oil shock keeps inflation expectations elevated, catalyzing a historic bond rout that peaked with the 30-year UST yield at 5.2% — its highest since 2007. A relief catalyst (Iran-US peace signals drove Brent −6% WoW) enabled a partial equity recovery; the S&P 500 ended the week +0.4%.

regime.label: Mixed → Risk-Off (Stagflationary)
regime.evidence: 30Y UST at 5.2%; 3 consecutive S&P 500 session losses mid-week; Silver −7%; gold declining despite war backdrop

events.title: Global Bond Rout — 30Y UST at 5.2% (19-year high)
events.reaction: S&P −3 consecutive sessions; Silver −7%; Multi-decade yield highs
events.detail: The US 30Y Treasury yield surged to 5.2% (highest since 2007) and the 10Y touched 4.67% on a triple driver: energy inflation, ~7%-of-GDP fiscal deficits, and expectations that incoming Fed Chair Warsh holds or tightens. Zero rate cuts are now priced through end-2027.

events.title: Iran War Oil −6% WoW Correction on Peace Talks
events.reaction: Brent $118→$110; Dow intraday record on Friday
events.detail: Brent fell ~6% WoW (to ~$110–112) on US signals of progress with Iran, though oil remains +52–60% since the Feb 28 war start. Iran's late-week move to keep enriched uranium reserves partially reversed the relief rally.

events.title: Japan 30Y JGB Yield at All-Time Record
events.reaction: Carry-trade risk activated; BOJ policy pressure amplified
events.detail: Japan's 30Y JGB yield hit an all-time record on May 18, amplifying the global bond rout. With three BOJ dissenters calling for a 1.0% hike and FY2026 core inflation raised to 2.8%, June BOJ is a live rate-hike catalyst with carry-trade unwind risk.

events.title: Kevin Warsh Sworn In as Federal Reserve Chair
events.reaction: Policy uncertainty premium; Hawkish shift risk
events.detail: Kevin Warsh was sworn in as the 17th Fed Chair on May 22 (confirmed 54-45, the narrowest margin in Fed chair history). Viewed as more hawkish than Powell, his first decision comes at the June 16-17 FOMC against a backdrop of an 8-4 April split and 30Y yields at 5.2%.

events.title: NVIDIA Q1 FY27 Earnings Beat — "Sell the News"
events.reaction: NVDA −0.5% despite $81.6B revenue beat; AI cycle intact
events.detail: NVIDIA posted record revenue of $81.6B (+85% YoY) with $91B Q2 guidance and a 25× dividend increase, yet the stock opened −0.5% — a classic 'sell the news' after a pre-earnings run-up, with macro headwinds overwhelming the beat.

catalysts: June BOJ Meeting · live rate-hike risk

brief.headline: The week was defined by a quadruple convergence: an inflationary data shock, a Fed leadership transition, trade diplomacy, and divergent mega-cap earnings. April CPI printed at a three-year high of 3.8% YoY on Iran War energy pass-through, forcing markets to abandon residual rate-cut expectations; the S&P 500 hit an intraday high of 7,501 before reversing −1.24% on Friday.

regime.label: Mixed → shifting Risk-Off by week-end
regime.evidence: Mid-week S&P and Nasdaq at ATHs and Dow above 50,000, but Friday close S&P −1.24% with deteriorating breadth and widening credit spreads

events.title: Iran War — Strait of Hormuz Oil Shock
events.reaction: WTI +11% weekly; Brent ~$106
events.detail: The US-Israel war has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz since late February, removing ~3.9M bpd with ~10.5M bpd of Gulf production offline; Aramco's CEO warned stability cannot return before 2027. Brent is ~$106, up ~47% since late February.

events.title: April CPI +3.8% YoY / PPI +6.0% YoY
events.reaction: S&P -1.24%; 10Y yields spike
events.detail: April CPI printed +0.6% MoM / +3.8% YoY (a three-year high) on Iran War energy pass-through, with core at +2.8% confirming broadening; PPI rose +6.0% YoY, the largest gain since December 2022, eliminating any near-term Fed cut case.

events.title: Trump-Xi Beijing Summit — 30% Tariff Truce
events.reaction: S&P ATH then -1.24% reversal
events.detail: Trump's two-day Beijing summit produced a tariff truce cutting US tariffs from 145% to 30% (through Aug 12) and China's to 10%, plus a 200-Boeing-jet order, but markets were disappointed by no breakthroughs on rare earths or export controls.

events.title: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings (Alphabet, Amazon, MSFT, Apple, NVIDIA)
events.reaction: Nasdaq ATH; Alphabet +10%; NVIDIA -8.4%
events.detail: Earnings diverged sharply: Alphabet surged +10% on Google Cloud +63% YoY and Apple rose +3.3% on strong guidance, while Microsoft fell -4% on a $190B capex projection and NVIDIA fell -8.4% over four days ahead of its May 20 report.

events.title: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as Fed Chair
events.reaction: Policy uncertainty repriced
events.detail: The Senate confirmed Warsh 54-45, the closest in modern Fed history, with Powell unusually retaining a board seat and vote. The same-day CPI print immediately constrained the rate cuts the White House expects, with markets pricing no 2026 cuts.

risks.label: White House pressure forces premature Warsh dovishness

brief.headline: Two counteracting forces defined the week: the 2026 Iran war / Strait of Hormuz supply shock — the largest oil disruption on record, removing ~14M barrels/day — and an AI-powered Magnificent Seven earnings superweek that drove the S&P 500 to a record close of 7,399 (+2.3% weekly) and Nasdaq to 26,247 (+4.5%). The dominant macro risk is stagflationary.

regime.label: Mixed — Risk-On in equities; elevated risk premium in energy/commodities
regime.evidence: VIX sub-20 and S&P at all-time high, but gold at $4,721 and Brent at $101 signal persistent macro anxiety

events.title: Iran War / Strait of Hormuz Supply Shock
events.reaction: Brent peaked $111 → $101; Gold $4,721; VIX elevated
events.detail: The Strait of Hormuz has remained largely closed since late February with the IEA estimating ~14M barrels/day removed; Brent retreated to ~$101 on ceasefire talks before US forces struck two Iranian tankers on May 8. Fertilizer prices are up 31% as a knock-on effect.

events.title: Mag-7 Tech Earnings Superweek
events.reaction: Nasdaq +4.5% wk; S&P 500 record 7,399
events.detail: Five Magnificent Seven members all beat consensus with cloud re-accelerating (Google Cloud +63% YoY); the four hyperscalers collectively pledged $600–645B in 2026 AI capex. Apple was the outlier with minimal capex.

events.title: FOMC 8-4 Historic Dissent Hold
events.reaction: Rates held 3.50–3.75%; most dissents since 1992
events.detail: The FOMC voted 8-4 to hold for a third consecutive meeting — the most dissents since October 1992 — with the median dot still projecting one cut. The Iran energy shock has created a genuine stagflationary dilemma.

events.title: US-China Tariffs 145%/125% (Ongoing)
events.reaction: Persistent drag; Trump-Xi summit imminent
events.detail: US tariffs on Chinese goods stand at 145% (via non-IEEPA authority after the February Supreme Court ruling) against China's 125% reciprocal rate, with a Trump-Xi summit imminent centering on a 500-Boeing-aircraft purchase and rare-earth access.

events.title: US April NFP +115K Beat
events.reaction: S&P +0.8% to record; Unemployment 4.3%
events.detail: April non-farm payrolls rose 115,000, beating the ~90,000 consensus, with unemployment holding at 4.3% — reinforcing the soft-landing narrative and pushing the S&P and Nasdaq to all-time highs.

brief.headline: Five market-moving events unfolded against the worst energy supply shock since 1973 colliding with the strongest US mega-cap earnings season since 2020. Equity markets staged a historic AI-driven rally (S&P 500 +10.4% in April, Nasdaq +15.3%) while energy markets remain structurally dislocated with WTI briefly touching $111/bbl.

regime.label: Mixed — Risk-On in equities; Risk-Off/Inflationary in macro
regime.evidence: Record equity highs with VIX compressed, but 10Y yield at 9-month highs and WTI peak $111 signal stagflationary stress

events.title: Strait of Hormuz / Middle East Oil Supply Shock
events.reaction: WTI peak $111/bbl; +60% cumulative since Feb
events.detail: The Middle East conflict disrupted the Strait of Hormuz (~35% of seaborne crude), triggering an estimated ~10 mb/d supply loss — the largest since the 1970s. The World Bank projects energy prices to surge 24% in 2026.

events.title: Mega-Cap Big Tech Q1 2026 Earnings Week
events.reaction: S&P 500 record 7,230; Nasdaq +15.3% for April
events.detail: All five reporting Magnificent Seven companies beat EPS consensus, validating AI monetization at scale; Alphabet rose +6% after-hours on 22% revenue growth and ~800% YoY generative AI revenue. Combined reporting market cap was ~$16 trillion.

events.title: FOMC Apr 28–29: Hold + Historic 4-Member Dissent
events.reaction: 10Y yield tested 4.45%; Warsh succession signal
events.detail: The FOMC voted 8-4 to hold at 3.50–3.75% — the most divided vote since October 1992 — citing Middle East uncertainty. Powell's tenure ends May 15, with the more hawkish Warsh chairing the June meeting.

events.title: ECB Apr 30: Hold + Stagflation Risk Acknowledged
events.reaction: Rate hike priced by June; EUR volatile
events.detail: The ECB held the refi rate at 2.15% but Lagarde formally acknowledged intensifying stagflation risk as flash CPI jumped to 3.0% and Q1 GDP grew just +0.1%. Markets now price three ECB hikes in 2026.

events.title: BOJ Apr 28: Hold + 3 Hawkish Dissents + Inflation Upgrade
events.reaction: JPY strengthened; BOJ June hike signaled
events.detail: The BOJ voted 6-3 to hold at 0.75% with three members favoring an immediate hike, while cutting FY2026 GDP to 0.5% and raising core CPI to 2.8% — a stagflationary revision implying a June hike consequential for carry trades.

catalysts: Powell's last day as Fed Chair · May 15

risks.label: Stagflation Confirmation — CPI Spike + Weak NFP
BOJ Emergency Hike or June Surprise

brief.headline: An active US-Israel-Iran conflict effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz is driving a historic energy shock (+13–18% oil weekly), while the approaching Magnificent 7 "super week" earnings (April 29–30) creates a bifurcated tape between energy/safe-haven assets and AI-driven mega-cap tech. Central banks (Fed, ECB, BOJ, BOE, BoC) are all expected to hold, removing monetary policy as a near-term catalyst.

regime.label: Mixed — Risk-Off in energy/macro, Risk-On in AI/tech (conditional)
regime.evidence: Energy spike + IMF recession language = risk-off for global growth-sensitive assets; safe-haven gold at multi-year highs

events.title: US-Iran War: Hormuz Closure & Ceasefire Fragility
events.reaction: Brent +17–18% wk; WTI +13% wk; Gold multi-yr high; USD soft
events.detail: Operation Epic Fury entered its eighth week with the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed; a fragile April 8 ceasefire was undercut by Iran attacking three commercial vessels on April 22. The IMF formally warned of global recession risk if disruptions persist.

events.title: Trump 50% China Tariff Threat (145% active rate)
events.reaction: Semi/tech under pressure; CNY weak; copper repriced
events.detail: Trump threatened an additional 50% tariff on China atop the existing 145% rate if Beijing supplies weapons to Iran, merging trade and military-alliance policy into a single instrument. The April 2 Proclamation also expanded Section 232 to copper.

events.title: Mag-7 Earnings Super Week: Tesla + Fwd MSFT/Meta/GOOGL/AMZN
events.reaction: Nasdaq direction held hostage to Apr 29–30 prints; VIX elevated
events.detail: Tesla reported April 22 and disclosed early talks for a large-scale AI compute facility expansion; the April 29–30 window brings the highest-concentration earnings event of Q1 2026, testing whether AI capex translates to revenue proof.

events.title: Fed Holds 3.50–3.75%; April 28–29 FOMC Approaching
events.reaction: 10yr ~4.3–4.5%; DXY ~98.5; 1 cut priced for late 2026
events.detail: The Fed held for a second consecutive meeting as March CPI surged to 3.3% YoY on oil-driven geopolitical inflation. Market pricing shows 99.7% probability of no change at the April 28–29 meeting.

events.title: ECB Hold + June Hike Pricing; "Layer Cake of Shocks"
events.reaction: EUR/USD ~1.1686; Bund yields rising; VSTOXX elevated
events.detail: The ECB held all three key rates as eurozone headline inflation jumped to 2.6% in March on the Iran energy shock. Lagarde warned of a "layer cake of shocks" with markets pricing a June hike despite 0.9% GDP growth.
